article_meta.title: By Jonah Goldberg July 11, 2016 11:14 AM PT Share via Close extra sharing options Email Facebook X LinkedIn Threads Redd

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for what is clearly an opinion column by Jonah Goldberg. While opinion columns are inherently opinionated and one could argue the model was being cautious, there are several instances of author-level bias in the prose that should have been flagged. The article contains multiple opinion statements presented as fact, subjective qualifying adjectives, and loaded word choices in the author's own voice. However, it's worth noting this is labeled as an opinion piece, which complicates the analysis somewhat - but the instructions don't exempt opinion pieces.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: No attribution violations since the model returned an empty array - nothing was flagged at all, so no quoted text was incorrectly attributed to the author.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No false positives since nothing was flagged. Score of 1 is appropriate as there are zero incorrect flags.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Multiple clear instances of author-level bias were missed. Examples: (1) 'knocked the lazy certainty out of almost everybody' - Subjective Qualifying Adjectives/Word Choice; (2) 'Rudy Giuliani (who did not lose his lazy certainty)' - Mudslinging/Ad Hominem or Subjective Qualifying Adjectives; (3) 'fairly irrelevant' - Opinion Statements Presented as Fact; (4) 'liberals are often rather obtuse in how they discuss policing' - Word Choice/Ad Hominem; (5) 'creating one of the strangest ironies of modern liberalism' - Opinion Statements Presented as Fact; (6) 'seemingly boundless faith in the power and nobility of government' - Word Choice/Spin; (7) 'nothing undermines the legitimacy of the law more than vilifying those sworn to uphold it' - Opinion Statements Presented as Fact. While this is an opinion column, these are clearly biased phrasings in the author's own prose.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No labels to evaluate since the model returned an empty array. Score of 1 by default.
